meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't collaborate (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Collaboration Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with fragmented workflows. Master the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to streamline client collaboration and ship faster.
content.answer_block: The 'can't collaborate' bottleneck stems from fragmented state management and siloed codebases. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you unify your architecture into a single, LLM-orchestrated source of truth. This empowers Claude Code to maintain context across your entire stack, eliminating manual synchronization errors and accelerating your development velocity significantly.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you’re three weeks into a high-stakes build, and the client suddenly demands a pivot. Your codebase is a labyrinth, your documentation is outdated, and the team is tripping over each other's commits. The friction of 'not collaborating' isn't just a nuisance; it's a revenue-killer that turns high-margin projects into maintenance nightmares.

But what if your project structure was inherently collaborative? By adopting the Antigravity project template, you transform your repository from a static collection of files into a living, AI-aware ecosystem. We’re moving beyond simple file structures; we’re architecting for machine-readable context, allowing you and your AI agents to move in lockstep, shipping production-grade features in record time.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the foundational directory structure that enforces strict separation of concerns, ensuring Claude Code has clear boundaries for its operations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize an Antigravity project structure with modular components, standardized API routes, and a centralized state management layer.

steps.title: Inject Contextual Metadata
steps.description: Populate the .ag-context files to provide your AI agents with the business logic and client requirements, turning them into project-aware assistants.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a comprehensive .ag-context file detailing core business logic and architectural constraints.

steps.title: Configure Shared State Schemas
steps.description: Define the source of truth for your data models to prevent drift during collaborative sessions between you and the AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified schema definition for the project state that ensures type safety across all frontend and backend interactions.

steps.title: Implement Agentic Guardrails
steps.description: Set up automated validation checks that prevent the AI from introducing regressions during rapid iterative builds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure agentic guardrails in the Antigravity template to validate code changes against the established project architecture before deployment.

steps.title: Execute Synchronized Iteration
steps.description: Run the collaborative loop where you and Claude Code iterate on features based on the validated architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review current progress and implement the next feature from the backlog, maintaining strict adherence to the Antigravity template standards.

pro_tips: Use custom .ag-ignore files to prevent Claude Code from indexing heavy binary assets, keeping your context window lean and fast.
Map your client's most frequent requests to custom 'shortcut' prompts within your Antigravity template for one-click feature generation.
Always perform a 'schema diff' using Claude Code after a major feature injection to ensure the AI hasn't hallucinated new, unrequested data types.
Leverage the Antigravity 'hot-reload' feature to instantly see how AI-generated code affects your live client environment.
Keep your repository documentation inside the /docs folder as Markdown; Claude Code will automatically ingest this to maintain semantic alignment during development.
resources: Best Practices for AI-Assisted Architecture: https://ai-builder.dev/patterns
content.next_steps: Once your template is live, focus on building a library of reusable 'Action Blocks' that you can inject into any future client project to reduce setup time by 80%.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your tools and start building the future of software. With the Antigravity template, you gain the leverage of a ten-person team in a single terminal window. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
